Concurrency

Subpage of Operating Systems

Phase 2 — Concurrency (Weeks 5–8). This is where most students struggle. Go slow on deadlock and memory consistency models. Implement a small thread library yourself (pthreads-style) to make the primitives concrete.

As you go along, you need to finish the following topics:

⚠️
Make a page for each topic as you go along and then delete this page once you are done.
Concurrency vs Parallelism
Threads — Model, Creation, Lifecycle
Locks and Mutexes
Semaphores
Condition Variables
Monitors and Higher-level Primitives
The Producer-Consumer Problem
Deadlock — Detection, Avoidance, Recovery
Starvation and Priority Inversion
Lock-Free Data Structures
Memory Consistency Models
Event-Driven vs Thread-Based Concurrency
Concurrency Bugs and Debugging
